text:
COMPREHENSIVE HEMATOLOGIC MALIGNANCY NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient Name: Renata Kowalczyk
Date of Birth: [DOB on file]
Sex: Female
MRN: [on file]
Ordering Physician: [Hematology/Oncology Attending]
Diagnosis/Indication: Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML), FAB M4, de novo; evaluation for somatic mutations, prognostication, and targeted therapy eligibility
Specimen Type: Bone marrow aspirate
Date of Collection: [at diagnosis]
Date Received: [on file]
Date Reported: [on file]
Accession Number: [on file]

Panel: ComprehensiveHeme-523 (523-gene hematologic malignancy panel; includes full coding exons, splice sites, select introns for fusion/ITD detection, and copy number analysis)
Sequencing Platform: Illumina NovaSeq 6000, paired-end 150 bp
Mean Coverage Depth: 1,247x (tumor); 312x (germline reference)
Tumor Purity (estimated): 78% (consistent with morphologic blast count of 74%)
Variant Allele Frequency (VAF) reporting threshold: >=2% (somatic); FLT3-ITD by fragment analysis
Microsatellite Instability (MSI): Assessed by panel-derived loci
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): Calculated from exonic somatic mutations

---

SECTION 1: SUMMARY OF FINDINGS

Two clinically significant somatic alterations identified:
  1. FLT3 internal tandem duplication (ITD), exon 14-15 junction, insertion length 48 bp, allelic ratio 0.82, VAF 82% (HIGH ALLELIC RATIO - Adverse prognostic significance per ELN 2022)
  2. DNMT3A p.R882H (c.2645G>A), missense mutation, VAF 47%

No additional pathogenic or likely pathogenic somatic variants identified in the 521 remaining genes analyzed.
No AML-defining recurrent chromosomal translocations or gene fusions detected by RNA-seq reflex testing.
No pathogenic copy number alterations detected.
Tumor Mutational Burden: 3 mutations/Mb (LOW)
Microsatellite status: Microsatellite Stable (MSS)

ELN 2022 Molecular Risk Classification: ADVERSE
  Basis: FLT3-ITD with high allelic ratio (>=0.5) in the absence of NPM1 mutation

---

SECTION 2: CLINICALLY ACTIONABLE / REPORTABLE VARIANTS

------------------------------------------------------------
GENE:        FLT3
Variant:     Internal Tandem Duplication (ITD)
Location:    Exon 14-15 juxtamembrane domain (JMD)
Insertion Length: 48 base pairs
Allelic Ratio: 0.82 (high; threshold for high AR: >=0.5 per ELN 2022)
VAF:         82%
Method:      Fragment length analysis (capillary electrophoresis) and confirmatory NGS read-pair analysis
Zygosity:    Cannot exclude homozygosity vs. high-AR heterozygosity; allelic ratio >0.5 supports high clonal burden
Classification: Pathogenic / Clinically Actionable
Clinical Significance:
  FLT3-ITD is the most common somatic mutation in AML, occurring in approximately 20-25% of de novo AML cases. Insertions within the juxtamembrane domain lead to constitutive autophosphorylation and activation of downstream pro-survival signaling pathways (PI3K/AKT, RAS/MAPK, STAT5). FLT3-ITD is associated with leukocytosis, high blast burden, and inferior outcomes with standard chemotherapy alone. The allelic ratio (AR) reflects the proportion of FLT3-ITD to wild-type FLT3 alleles; high AR (>=0.5) confers a particularly adverse prognosis and is an independent predictor of relapse and reduced overall survival. This patient's AR of 0.82 places her in the highest-risk FLT3-ITD subgroup.
  Per ELN 2022 guidelines, FLT3-ITD with high AR in the absence of co-occurring NPM1 mutation is classified as ELN Adverse risk, strongly supporting consideration of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (alloHSCT) in first complete remission (CR1).
  FLT3 inhibitors (midostaurin, gilteritinib, quizartinib) are approved or have demonstrated efficacy in FLT3-mutant AML. The RATIFY trial established midostaurin (50 mg BID, days 8-21) in combination with standard 7+3 induction as standard of care in newly diagnosed FLT3-mutant AML patients aged <=60 years, with a significant improvement in overall survival (HR 0.78, p=0.009). Gilteritinib is approved in the relapsed/refractory setting (ADMIRAL trial).
  Note: FLT3 point mutation (D835/I836, TKD) was not detected (see Section 3). Concurrent FLT3-TKD mutation is not present.
------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------
GENE:        DNMT3A
Variant:     p.R882H (c.2645G>A)
Exon:        Exon 23
VAF:         47%
Depth:       1,183x (563 alt / 1,183 total reads)
Zygosity:    Heterozygous
Classification: Pathogenic
Clinical Significance:
  DNMT3A encodes DNA methyltransferase 3A, an enzyme essential for de novo DNA methylation. The p.R882H hotspot substitution is the most common DNMT3A mutation in AML, accounting for approximately 60-70% of all DNMT3A mutations in myeloid malignancies. The R882 residue is critical for the catalytic activity and oligomerization of DNMT3A; the p.R882H variant acts in a dominant-negative fashion, reducing global DNMT3A methyltransferase activity by approximately 80%.
  DNMT3A R882 mutations occur in approximately 18-22% of de novo AML and are frequently observed as co-mutations with FLT3-ITD (the so-called FLT3-ITD/DNMT3A/NPM1 "triple" and FLT3-ITD/DNMT3A "double" mutant AML subsets). In the absence of NPM1 co-mutation (as in this case), FLT3-ITD/DNMT3A double-mutant AML retains its ELN Adverse classification and is associated with inferior survival outcomes.
  DNMT3A R882H is also a well-characterized clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) mutation and may persist at low VAF after successful AML therapy, representing residual non-malignant CHIP rather than residual disease. The VAF of 47% at diagnosis is consistent with a co-driver role in the leukemic clone (approximately diploid locus, one allele mutated) and contributes to the founding clonal architecture.
  No currently approved therapies specifically target DNMT3A R882H in AML; however, hypomethylating agents (azacitidine, decitabine) have mechanistic rationale and clinical activity in DNMT3A-mutant AML, particularly in the maintenance or salvage setting.
------------------------------------------------------------

---

SECTION 3: GENES ASSESSED - WILD-TYPE (NO PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS DETECTED)

The following genes were fully sequenced with adequate coverage (mean coverage >500x; all reportable exons covered at >=100x). No pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants were identified:

AML CORE MUTATIONS / PROGNOSTIC MARKERS:
  NPM1          Wild-type (no exon 12 insertion/duplication detected by NGS and confirmatory fragment analysis)
  CEBPA         Wild-type (no bZIP or TAD domain mutations; biallelic CEBPA not present)
  FLT3-TKD      Wild-type (D835, I836 - no point mutations in activation loop)
  IDH1          Wild-type (R132 - no hotspot mutation)
  IDH2          Wild-type (R140, R172 - no hotspot mutations)
  KIT           Wild-type (D816, exon 8 - no activating mutations)
  WT1           Wild-type
  RUNX1         Wild-type (no mutations in Runt homology domain or transactivation domain)
  TP53          Wild-type (no pathogenic variants in exons 4-10 or splice sites)

EPIGENETIC REGULATORS / CHROMATIN REMODELING:
  TET2          Wild-type
  ASXL1         Wild-type (no truncating frameshift or nonsense variants)
  EZH2          Wild-type
  KDM6A         Wild-type
  KDM2B         Wild-type

SIGNAL TRANSDUCTION:
  NRAS          Wild-type (G12, G13, Q61 - no activating mutations)
  KRAS          Wild-type (G12, G13, Q61 - no activating mutations)
  PTPN11        Wild-type (exons 3, 13 - no activating mutations)
  CBL           Wild-type
  NF1           Wild-type

SPLICEOSOME MUTATIONS:
  SF3B1         Wild-type
  SRSF2         Wild-type
  U2AF1         Wild-type
  ZRSR2         Wild-type

COHESIN COMPLEX:
  STAG2         Wild-type
  RAD21         Wild-type
  SMC1A         Wild-type
  SMC3          Wild-type

DNA DAMAGE RESPONSE / REPAIR:
  ATM           Wild-type
  ATR           Wild-type
  BRCA1         Wild-type
  BRCA2         Wild-type
  PALB2         Wild-type

OTHER AML-RELEVANT GENES (select):
  BCOR          Wild-type
  BCORL1        Wild-type
  MLL/KMT2A     Wild-type (no partial tandem duplication detected; no rearrangement by FISH)
  MLL2/KMT2D    Wild-type
  SETBP1        Wild-type
  PHF6          Wild-type
  GATA2         Wild-type
  GATA1         Wild-type
  CSF3R         Wild-type
  CALR          Wild-type
  JAK2          Wild-type (V617F and exon 12 negative)
  MPL           Wild-type
  ETNK1         Wild-type
  GNB1          Wild-type
  DDX41         Wild-type (germline DDX41 mutation excluded)
  ANKRD26       Wild-type
  CEBPB         Wild-type
  IRF1          Wild-type
  CUX1          Wild-type
  ETV6          Wild-type
  IKZF1         Wild-type
  PAX5          Wild-type

Note: A total of 523 genes were assessed. Only medically significant findings are reported in Sections 2 and 3. Complete gene list available upon request.

---

SECTION 4: GENE FUSION / STRUCTURAL VARIANT ANALYSIS

RNA-seq and/or anchored multiplex PCR performed as reflexive component of the hematologic malignancy panel for detection of recurrent AML-defining fusions and structural rearrangements.

AML-DEFINING FUSIONS / REARRANGEMENTS ASSESSED:
  PML-RARA (t(15;17)(q24.1;q21.2)):          NOT DETECTED
  RUNX1-RUNX1T1 (t(8;21)(q22;q22.1)):        NOT DETECTED
  CBFB-MYH11 (inv(16)(p13.1q22) / t(16;16)): NOT DETECTED
  DEK-NUP214 (t(6;9)(p23;q34.1)):            NOT DETECTED
  BCR-ABL1 (t(9;22)(q34;q11.2)):             NOT DETECTED
  KMT2A/MLL rearrangement (11q23):            NOT DETECTED (11 partner genes assessed by RT-PCR and NGS fusion algorithm)
  EVI1/MECOM rearrangement (3q26.2):          NOT DETECTED
  NUP98-NSD1 (t(5;11)(q35.2;p15.4)):         NOT DETECTED
  NUP98-KDM5A (t(11;12)(p15.4;p13.3)):       NOT DETECTED
  RBM15-MRTFA (t(1;22)(p13.3;q13.1)):        NOT DETECTED
  GATA2-MECOM (inv(3)(q21.3q26.2)):          NOT DETECTED

Additional fusions screened: 127 additional recurrent hematologic malignancy fusions assessed; no fusions detected.

FLT3-ITD CONFIRMATION (independent assay):
  FLT3-ITD: DETECTED - insertion at exon 14-15 junction, 48 bp insertion; allelic ratio 0.82 by fragment analysis (capillary electrophoresis)
  FLT3-TKD (D835/I836): NOT DETECTED

---

SECTION 5: COPY NUMBER ANALYSIS

Copy number alterations (CNAs) were assessed across all 523 panel genes and genome-wide from NGS read-depth analysis.

No pathogenic somatic copy number alterations identified.
No gene amplifications detected.
No homozygous deletions detected.
No arm-level or whole-chromosome gains/losses detected.

Normalized copy number: Diploid throughout assessed regions.

Correlation with conventional cytogenetics: Normal karyotype 46,XX confirmed by G-banding [20 metaphases analyzed] - concordant with NGS copy number analysis.

FISH Panel Results (performed in parallel by cytogenetics laboratory):
  t(8;21)/RUNX1-RUNX1T1:         Negative
  inv(16)/t(16;16)/CBFB-MYH11:   Negative
  t(15;17)/PML-RARA:              Negative
  t(9;22)/BCR-ABL1:               Negative
  KMT2A/MLL (11q23) rearrangement: Negative
  EVI1/MECOM (3q26.2) rearrangement: Negative
  del(5q):                         Negative
  del(7q) / monosomy 7:           Negative
  trisomy 8:                       Negative

---

SECTION 6: TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN AND MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY

Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3 mutations/Mb
  Classification: LOW (threshold for TMB-High: >=10 mut/Mb)
  Note: AML characteristically displays low TMB. This result is consistent with the expected mutational burden for de novo AML and does not suggest mismatch repair deficiency or hypermutator phenotype.

Microsatellite Instability (MSI): Microsatellite Stable (MSS)
  17 microsatellite loci assessed; 0/17 unstable loci detected.

---

SECTION 7: GERMLINE CONSIDERATIONS

This analysis was performed on tumor (bone marrow aspirate) DNA only. A paired normal (germline) sample was not submitted for this analysis.

Germline assessment note:
  - DNMT3A R882H at VAF 47% is most consistent with a somatic mutation in the leukemic clone at approximately heterozygous allele frequency, given the estimated tumor purity of 78%. Germline DNMT3A mutations have been reported but are rare; germline origin is not favored given the somatic VAF and clinical presentation. Germline confirmation may be considered if clinically indicated (e.g., family history of myeloid malignancy).
  - DDX41: Wild-type at all known pathogenic germline positions (p.D140Gfs*2, p.R525H, p.M1I, and others); germline DDX41 mutation is excluded as a predisposing factor to the best resolution of this tumor-only assay. If concern remains, germline testing is recommended.
  - RUNX1, ANKRD26, ETV6, CEBPA: Wild-type at all known germline predisposition loci. No evidence of familial platelet disorder or hereditary AML predisposition syndrome.
  - BRCA1/BRCA2/PALB2: No pathogenic variants detected; cancer susceptibility implications minimal in the context of AML.
  - Note: This tumor-only panel cannot fully exclude germline variants. For definitive germline assessment, a dedicated germline testing panel on a non-hematopoietic specimen (e.g., buccal swab, skin fibroblast) is recommended if clinically indicated.

---

SECTION 8: INTERPRETATION AND MOLECULAR SUMMARY

This comprehensive 523-gene NGS panel performed on bone marrow aspirate from a 48-year-old female with de novo AML (FAB M4, acute myelomonocytic leukemia) identifies two somatic driver mutations:

1. FLT3-ITD (exon 14-15, 48 bp insertion, allelic ratio 0.82): This is the principal oncogenic driver and primary therapeutic target in this patient. The high allelic ratio indicates either acquisition of a second FLT3-ITD allele, loss of heterozygosity at the FLT3 locus with retention of the mutant allele, or a very high clonal frequency of FLT3-ITD-bearing leukemic cells. FLT3-ITD with high AR (>=0.5) in the absence of NPM1 mutation confers ELN 2022 Adverse risk, which is associated with a 4-year overall survival of approximately 25-35% with chemotherapy alone (historical data, pre-FLT3 inhibitor era) and substantially improved outcomes when an allogeneic stem cell transplant is performed in CR1. The addition of midostaurin to induction chemotherapy (RATIFY regimen) is the standard of care for this mutation.

2. DNMT3A p.R882H (VAF 47%): This co-mutation is a known epigenetic driver in AML, co-occurring with FLT3-ITD in approximately 10-15% of cases. As a dominant-negative inhibitor of DNMT3A methyltransferase activity, it leads to focal loss of DNA methylation and transcriptional dysregulation at hematopoietic regulatory loci. DNMT3A R882H is also a CHIP mutation and may persist at very low VAF following successful induction chemotherapy as a residual CHIP clone independent of AML eradication; this must be distinguished from residual AML at MRD assessment.

Molecular profile in the context of AML classification (WHO 2022 / ELN 2022):
  - AML classification: AML, not otherwise specified (NOS) / AML with FLT3-ITD (per 2022 ICC: AML defined by FLT3-ITD)
  - No NPM1 mutation: absent
  - No core binding factor (CBF) rearrangements: absent - confirms non-CBF AML
  - No TP53 mutation: favorable
  - Normal karyotype: not sufficient to shift risk from Adverse in the setting of FLT3-ITD high AR without NPM1

Overall: Molecularly defined de novo AML with FLT3-ITD (high allelic ratio) and DNMT3A R882H co-mutation, normal karyotype, ELN 2022 Adverse risk. The molecular profile strongly supports the use of a FLT3 inhibitor (midostaurin) in combination with induction chemotherapy and proceeding to allogeneic stem cell transplantation in first complete remission.

---

SECTION 9: TECHNICAL NOTES AND LIMITATIONS

- DNA extraction from bone marrow aspirate; specimen quality adequate (DNA integrity number >7.0; 260/280 ratio 1.84).
- Library preparation: SureSelect XT HS2 hybrid capture; paired-end sequencing.
- FLT3-ITD detection was performed by capillary electrophoresis fragment analysis (gold standard for ITD detection and AR quantification) in addition to NGS-based detection. Fragment analysis is the validated method for FLT3-ITD allelic ratio determination.
- FLT3-TKD (D835/I836) was assessed by both NGS and confirmatory restriction fragment length polymorphism (RFLP) assay; TKD mutation not detected.
- This assay detects somatic variants at VAF >=2% at standard sequencing depth; low-level variants below this threshold may not be detected.
- Copy number analysis is limited in regions of low complexity or near centromeres/telomeres; conventional karyotyping and FISH provide orthogonal confirmation.
- Germline variants cannot be excluded without a paired normal sample. Variants at low VAF or consistent with germline frequency (VAF ~50% or ~100%) should be interpreted with caution without germline confirmation.
- RNA-seq fusion analysis requires adequate RNA quality; RIN >=6.0 confirmed for this sample.
- Results should be interpreted in conjunction with morphologic, immunophenotypic, cytogenetic, and clinical findings.

Reporting Pathologist / Molecular Geneticist: [Attending Hematopathologist / Laboratory Director]
Laboratory Director: [name on file, MD, PhD, FACMG]
CAP/CLIA Accredited Laboratory

  5. Arber DA et al. The 2022 International Consensus Classification of myeloid neoplasms and acute leukemias. Blood 2022;140(11):1200-1228.

--- END OF REPORT ---
